_meta.notes: ONLY 1 record accepts tv_series — TV circuit (Series Mania, Canneseries, Banff, Monte-Carlo) is a BLOCKING expansion for episodic reports; 165 bafta_qualifying nulls outstanding

records.name: Sundance Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Opens ~10 months before Jan festival dates; early/regular/late submission windows
records.budget_notes: Agnostic — historically championed no/low-budget breakthrough work alongside larger indies
records.notes: The premier US launchpad for independent film — real, direct pipeline to acquisition by distributors including A24, Neon, Magnolia (confirmed via Media Play News/Film Impact Georgia distributor interviews).

records.name: Cannes Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions open ~Jan/Feb for May festival; strict recency requirement
records.budget_notes: Agnostic, though Official Selection skews toward established/mid-to-high budget work
records.notes: World's most prestigious festival and adjoining market (Marche du Film) — major international sales/distribution hub.

records.name: Berlin International Film Festival (Berlinale)
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions open autumn prior year for Feb festival
records.notes: A-list festival with a real, adjoining market (EFM) heavily used by distributors and sales agents.

records.name: Toronto International Film Festival (TIFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions open spring for Sept festival
records.budget_notes: Agnostic — People's Choice Award is a real Oscar predictor
records.notes: Major North American market and sales venue, strong platform for African diaspora and international co-production narratives.

records.name: SXSW (South by Southwest)
records.deadline_pattern: Opens autumn prior year for March festival
records.budget_notes: Strong track record with genre and comedy breakthroughs specifically
records.notes: Real, confirmed as a distributor-scouted festival (Media Play News). Strong fit for genre-forward and tech-adjacent narratives.

records.name: Africa International Film Festival (AFRIFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Feature submissions typically close ~2 months before Nov festival
records.notes: Nigeria's premier festival — natural fit for Lagos-set or Nollywood-adjacent narratives.

records.name: Durban International Film Festival (DIFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Feature submissions typically close ~3 months before Jul festival
records.notes: Africa's premier pan-African festival, strong for African diaspora/co-production narratives.

records.name: Frameline (San Francisco International LGBTQ+ Film Festival)
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically open ~Jan for June festival
records.notes: The longest-running queer film festival in the world (confirmed via Women Make Movies distributor resource page) — real, established platform for LGBTQ+ narratives.

records.name: Outfest Los Angeles
records.deadline_pattern: Rolling, multiple deadlines through spring
records.notes: Major LA-based LGBTQ+ festival with real industry development programs (Screenwriting Lab, Producers Lab) alongside screenings.

records.name: International Queer Women of Color Film Festival (QWOCFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Free submission window through Dec 31, small late fee through mid-Jan
records.budget_notes: Explicitly considers access to industry resources — built for filmmakers with fewer prior opportunities
records.notes: Requires films be created by LBTQIA+ BIPOC filmmakers specifically (confirmed via the festival's own submission page) — free to submit, accepts films up to 3 years old.

records.name: Bentonville Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically open autumn prior year for June festival
records.notes: Chaired by Geena Davis, explicit mission for inclusive storytelling — films by/about women, non-binary, LGBTQIA+, BIPOC, and disabled people (confirmed via Women Make Movies).

records.name: Athena Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically open autumn for spring festival
records.notes: Barnard College-hosted festival centered on stories of women leaders — real, established (9+ years, 30,000+ attendees per Women Make Movies).

records.name: London Film Festival (BFI LFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically close June for Oct festival
records.notes: UK's premier festival, strong awards-season positioning, natural UK premiere platform for UK-shot or UK-co-produced work.

records.name: Fantastic Fest
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically close spring for Sept festival
records.notes: Confirmed via Wikipedia as one of the three largest/most prestigious genre festivals globally (with Sitges and Fantasia).

records.name: SITGES — International Fantastic Film Festival of Catalonia
records.deadline_pattern: Rolling deadlines through the year, festival held October
records.notes: Confirmed via Wikipedia — one of the three largest/most prestigious genre festivals globally, founded 1968.

records.name: Fantasia International Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Last deadline mid-April for July/Aug festival
records.notes: Confirmed via Adrian Tofei's genre festival guide and Wikipedia — Montreal-based, one of the three largest genre festivals globally.

records.name: FrightFest (London)
records.deadline_pattern: Rolling submissions, festival held August (London) + separate Glasgow edition
records.notes: Confirmed via Adrian Tofei's genre guide, founded 2000, real UK horror-specific platform.

records.name: Molins Horror Film Festival (TerrorMolins)
records.deadline_pattern: Early Jul, Regular Oct, Late Dec, Extended Jan deadlines
records.notes: Founded 1973, near Barcelona — confirmed via Adrian Tofei's genre festival guide.

records.name: Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically close Dec for April/May festival
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — largest documentary film festival in North America, strong industry attendance.

records.name: DOC NYC
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically close summer for Nov festival
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — America's largest documentary festival, active industry community.

records.name: Annecy International Animated Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Submissions typically close Feb for June festival
records.notes: Confirmed via Wikipedia — the oldest (founded 1960) and most prestigious animation-dedicated festival globally.

records.name: Hiroshima International Animation Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Biennial — verify current cycle directly
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — major international animation showcase with strong artistic/experimental focus. NOTE (2026-07): the Academy's 98th qualifying list names the successor event 'Hiroshima Animation Season' — this record needs review/renaming before the flag can be set.

records.name: HollyShorts Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Jan 30 (Early, $55) through June 1 (Extended, $85)
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — Oscar-qualifying in 4 categories (Best Short, Best Live Action, Best Animation, Best Documentary Short), requires LA premiere.

records.name: Palm Springs International ShortFest
records.deadline_pattern: Verify current cycle directly
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — largest short-film-focused festival in North America, 300+ films annually.

records.name: Seattle International Film Festival (SIFF)
records.deadline_pattern: Festival held May, submissions close prior winter
records.budget_notes: Agnostic; real launchpad for films lacking US distribution
records.notes: Confirmed via IndieWire — 2026 edition showcased 203 films from 71 countries, explicit focus on diverse voices/regions/storytelling.

records.name: Chicago International Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Festival held October
records.notes: Confirmed via Wikipedia — North America's longest-running competitive film festival (founded 1964), 175+ films from 50+ countries, includes a dedicated Black Perspectives section.

records.name: Ann Arbor Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Festival dates late March
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — 60+ years running, Oscar-qualifying, ~$38,000 in prizes, ideal for genre-blurring experimental work.

records.name: Oberhausen International Short Film Festival
records.deadline_pattern: Verify current cycle directly
records.notes: Confirmed via CMS Productions — one of the oldest and most respected platforms for experimental/avant-garde short work in Europe.

records.deadline_pattern: Verify current submission windows on the official site — not yet independently checked
